artPrompt: An evocative scene of ongoing prophecy: a hooded ashen prophet stands beneath a vast looming clock tower, arms outstretched as cold-gold sigils of dice and coins spiral upward through the smoke around their silhouette into a blood-dark sky. Guttering candles ring their feet in the gloom. A single divine shaft of pale gold light pierces the dark, rendered as a moody oil painting — old-master chiaroscuro, visible impasto brushwork, deep shadow, warm varnished tones.
name: Predict the Unpredictable
text:
Prophecy (When you're told to make a Prophecy, predict the named result out loud before the roll or flip.)
The first time each turn you make a Prophecy and are correct, draw a card and remove a doom counter from the Doomsday Clock.
The first time each turn you make a Prophecy and are wrong, you may pay {2}. If you do, the prophecy does not fail — refuse the future and try again next time. Otherwise, put a doom counter on the Doomsday Clock.
flavorText: Step one of accurate prophecy: lower your standards for "accurate."
